title: Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners
summary: The UN CC:Learn Open Online Course on Gender and Environment is currently accepting enrollments from learners around the world. This comprehensive self-paced course provides participants with an in-depth understanding of the relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability , while equipping them with practical tools to integrate gender-responsive approaches into environmental policies, projects, and development initiatives. Developed through a collaboration between leading international organizations, including the Global Environment Facility (GEF) , the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) , and UNITAR s UN CC:Learn , the course serves as an important learning resource for individuals seeking to strengthen their knowledge of climate action, biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and environmental governance. Unlike traditional classroom programs, this online course allows learners to study at their own pace from anywhere in the world. Participants can access learning materials whenever convenient, making it suitable for both students and working professionals. Why This Course Matters Environmental challenges such as climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and waste management do not affect everyone equally. Women, men, girls, and boys often experience environmental issues differently because of varying social, economic, and cultural roles. Understanding these differences is becoming increasingly important for governments, NGOs, development agencies, researchers, and businesses seeking to design inclusive environmental policies and sustainable development strategies. This course helps participants understand: Why gender equality contributes to better environmental outcomes. How environmental degradation affects different populations differently. The importance of women s leadership and empowerment in climate action. How gender-responsive policies improve conservation and sustainable development. International frameworks that support gender equality in environmental governance. What You Will Learn The course provides practical and theoretical knowledge covering several major environmental themes. Participants will learn: The relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability. Global environmental agreements that include gender considerations. How gender-responsive policies improve environmental outcomes. Practical approaches to integrating gender into environmental projects. Ways to promote women s empowerment within biodiversity conservation. Gender considerations in climate change adaptation and mitigation. Gender issues relating to international waters. Sustainable land degradation management. Chemicals and waste management from a gender perspective. Course Structure The learning programme consists of six comprehensive modules , each designed to build practical knowledge step by step. The modules include: Introduction Climate Change International Waters Biodiversity Land Degradation Chemicals and Waste Each module takes approximately one hour to complete, resulting in a total learning time of around six hours . Since the course is self-paced, learners can complete it according to their own schedules without fixed deadlines. Who Should Enroll? The course has been designed for a wide variety of learners, including: Students studying environmental sciences or sustainable development. Climate change practitioners. Biodiversity specialists. Government officials working on environmental policies. NGO staff involved in environmental conservation. Development practitioners. Researchers. Community leaders. Sustainability consultants. Anyone interested in learning how gender equality contributes to environmental sustainability. Certification Participants who successfully complete all quizzes with a minimum score of 70% qualify for a UN Digital Certificate of Completion . Important certification information: A minimum score of 70% is required on every quiz. Participants have three attempts for each quiz. The course itself is free. The official digital certificate becomes available upon payment of a USD 20 certification fee after successfully passing all quizzes. Languages Available The course is available in multiple languages, making it accessible to learners worldwide: English Spanish French International Partners The course has been developed through collaboration among several globally recognized organizations, including: Global Environment Facility (GEF) United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) UN CC:Learn International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) UN Women United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) Convention on Biological Diversity United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) Basel, Rotterdam and Stockholm Conventions, among others. Benefits of Taking the Course Participants will benefit from: Free access to high-quality UN learning materials. Flexible self-paced online learning. Practical knowledge applicable in environmental and development work. Improved understanding of global gender and environmental frameworks. Enhanced professional skills in sustainability and climate action. Opportunity to earn a recognized UN Digital Certificate. Better preparation for careers in environmental policy, NGOs, international organizations, research, and sustainable development. Application Deadline There is currently no application deadline. Enrollment remains open, and interested learners may register and begin the course at any time. How to Apply Interested applicants can register online through the official UN CC:Learn course page : Discover more global short courses opportunities on OFY here The post Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support
summary: Young leaders passionate about democracy, governance, public policy, and international affairs now have an outstanding opportunity to gain first-hand experience inside one of Europe s most influential democratic institutions. Applications are officially open for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026 , an initiative of the German Bundestag that enables outstanding young graduates to spend five months in Berlin while learning about Germany s parliamentary system and participating directly in legislative work. The International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) is one of Germany s flagship international exchange programmes designed to strengthen democratic values, encourage civic engagement, and foster long-term cooperation between Germany and partner countries. Established in 1986 , the programme has provided thousands of young professionals from approximately 50 countries with practical parliamentary experience while building an international network of future leaders committed to democracy and public service. For applicants in the United States, the programme is coordinated through the German Missions and offers selected participants the unique opportunity to work alongside a Member of the German Bundestag while also taking part in an extensive academic and professional development programme. About the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) The International Parliamentary Scholarship is organized by the German Bundestag in cooperation with three of Germany s leading universities: Freie Universität Berlin Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin Technische Universität Berlin Together, these institutions deliver a comprehensive programme that combines academic learning, professional training, seminars, networking opportunities, and practical parliamentary experience. The fellowship is specifically designed for talented young graduates who have demonstrated a commitment to democratic principles and civic participation in their home countries. Through the programme, participants gain valuable insights into parliamentary decision-making, political institutions, governance structures, and international cooperation. What Makes the IPS Programme Unique? Unlike traditional academic scholarships, the IPS Programme offers participants the opportunity to experience politics from inside the German Parliament. Participants spend five months in Berlin , where they become immersed in Germany s democratic institutions and political culture. One of the highlights of the programme is a three-month internship placement with a Member of the German Bundestag , allowing fellows to observe parliamentary work closely while contributing to daily legislative activities. This practical experience is complemented by university courses, professional seminars, workshops, and political training sessions that deepen participants understanding of democratic governance. The programme aims not only to educate participants but also to encourage international dialogue and strengthen democratic cooperation between Germany and participating countries. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows receive a comprehensive financial package that allows them to fully concentrate on learning and professional development throughout the programme. Benefits include: Monthly scholarship of 700 Euros Free accommodation throughout the fellowship Travel expenses Health insurance coverage Practical experience working in the German Parliament Participation in seminars and professional training sessions University courses delivered by leading Berlin universities Networking opportunities with political leaders, scholars, and fellow participants from around the world Exposure to Germany s parliamentary decision-making processes International leadership and intercultural experience Programme Duration The International Parliamentary Scholarship runs annually from: March 1 to July 31 During these five months, participants complete academic, professional, and practical parliamentary activities designed to prepare them for future leadership roles in government, public administration, international organizations, academia, civil society, and policy development. Eligibility Requirements Applicants from the United States must meet all of the following requirements: Hold U.S. citizenship Possess a completed college degree Demonstrate very good German language proficiency (minimum B2 level) Have a good understanding of German politics, society, and history Be under the age of 30 at the beginning of the fellowship Demonstrate a genuine interest in democratic governance and parliamentary systems Show active engagement in promoting democratic values and civil society The programme is particularly suited to graduates interested in careers in diplomacy, public administration, political science, international relations, law, public policy, journalism, governance, and civic leadership. Selection Process Applications undergo a competitive pre-selection process. Shortlisted candidates are invited to participate in interviews conducted within the United States. Final scholarship recipients are selected by an independent panel appointed by the German Bundestag. The rigorous selection process ensures that successful applicants possess strong academic credentials, leadership potential, commitment to democratic values, and the ability to serve as ambassadors for international cooperation. Why You Should Apply Participating in the International Parliamentary Scholarship offers far more than international study. It provides an opportunity to: Gain real-world parliamentary experience Build international leadership skills Develop professional networks across Germany and beyond Learn directly from legislators and political experts Enhance intercultural communication skills Strengthen your understanding of democratic institutions Improve your career prospects in government, international organizations, NGOs, academia, and public policy The IPS Programme has become a globally respected fellowship because it combines academic excellence with practical political experience, creating future leaders who contribute to democratic development in their own countries. Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026 is: 31 July 2025 Interested applicants should prepare their documents well before the deadline to ensure they have sufficient time to complete the application process. How to Apply Applicants should review the official programme information and submit their applications through the German Bundestag s official IPS application portal . Click here for Official Programme Information: Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNESCO Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences Job in India 2026: Apply for a 12-Month Position in New Delhi Before 26 July
summary: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) has announced an exciting career opportunity for professionals interested in advancing social development, gender equality, and human rights through international cooperation. Applications are now open for the Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences position based in New Delhi, India , offering successful candidates the opportunity to contribute to high-impact projects that empower communities and promote inclusive development. This position is hosted by UNESCO s Social and Human Sciences (SHS) Sector , one of the organization s key programme areas dedicated to promoting social inclusion, ethics, gender equality, youth empowerment, human rights, and peaceful societies. The successful candidate will support the implementation of the UNESCO–NIKE Kickstart Her Future – India Project , alongside other extra-budgetary initiatives within the SHS Sector. The appointment is offered under a Service Contract (SC6) for an initial duration of 12 months , with the duty station located in New Delhi, India . The vacancy is open to external candidates , providing professionals from relevant backgrounds with an opportunity to join one of the world s leading United Nations agencies. About UNESCO UNESCO is a specialized agency of the United Nations that works to build peace through international cooperation in education, science, culture, communication, and the social and human sciences. Through its programmes, UNESCO supports sustainable development, promotes human rights, strengthens education systems, preserves cultural heritage, and advances scientific collaboration across the globe. About the Project The selected Project Associate will play an important role in supporting the implementation of the UNESCO–NIKE Kickstart Her Future – India initiative. The project focuses on empowering women and girls by strengthening opportunities for participation, leadership, and social inclusion through collaborative programmes and partnerships. Working under the supervision of the Programme Specialist and Chief of the Social and Human Sciences Sector, the successful candidate will provide programme, administrative, financial, procurement, and coordination support to ensure successful project delivery. Key Responsibilities Successful applicants will contribute to several important functions, including: Supporting programme planning and implementation. Coordinating project activities and monitoring progress. Assisting with financial administration and budget tracking. Supporting procurement processes and contract administration. Organizing workshops, stakeholder consultations, meetings, and project events. Preparing reports, correspondence, presentations, and project documentation. Maintaining project records and ensuring timely reporting. Liaising with government institutions, implementing partners, NGOs, consultants, and other stakeholders. Supporting communication among project partners. Performing other administrative and coordination duties assigned by supervisors. Why You Should Apply Working with UNESCO provides professionals with an opportunity to contribute directly to international development while building valuable experience within the United Nations system. Some of the advantages of this position include: Opportunity to work with a globally respected UN agency. Professional experience in international development programmes. Exposure to large-scale gender equality and social inclusion initiatives. Collaboration with government institutions, civil society organizations, and international partners. Development of project management, financial management, procurement, and stakeholder engagement skills. Opportunity to contribute to projects supporting women and youth empowerment. International networking opportunities with development professionals. Required Qualifications Applicants should possess: A university degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Development Studies, Gender Studies, Public Administration, Management, Business Administration, International Relations, Finance, Commerce, or related disciplines. At least three years of relevant professional experience in programme management, project administration, finance, procurement, coordination, or related work. Excellent English communication skills. Strong organizational and analytical abilities. Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications. Ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders and multicultural teams. Core Values UNESCO seeks candidates who demonstrate its core organizational values: Commitment to the Organization Integrity Respect for Diversity Professionalism Contract Details The successful candidate will receive: Position: Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences Contract Type: Service Contract (SC6) Duration: 12 months Duty Station: New Delhi, India Work Arrangement: On-site Hiring Unit: Social and Human Sciences Sector (SHS) Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 26 July 2026 (Midnight UTC−5). How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official UNESCO Careers portal before the application deadline. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the job description, eligibility requirements, and required application documents before submitting their applications on official website . title: ACLU Strategy & Innovation Advisor Job 2026: Earn $142,694 Per Year Working on Civil Rights Strategy in New York or Washington, DC
summary: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU), one of the most influential civil rights organizations in the United States, is inviting applications for the position of Strategy Innovation Advisor within its National Office. This exciting opportunity is designed for recent graduate-level degree holders who are passionate about strategy, organizational leadership, innovation, public policy, and advancing civil rights and civil liberties. Based in either New York, NY or Washington, DC , this is a full-time hybrid position requiring employees to work in the office two days per week (or eight days each month) . The appointment is offered as a one-year term-limited position , providing successful candidates with an exceptional opportunity to contribute to one of the nation s most respected nonprofit organizations while gaining valuable leadership and strategic planning experience. With an annual salary of $142,694 for employees based in New York, this role is among the most competitive early-career leadership opportunities available within the nonprofit and advocacy sector. About the ACLU For more than 100 years , the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) has defended and preserved the rights and freedoms guaranteed by the U.S. Constitution and federal laws. The organization has played a leading role in landmark legal cases and public policy initiatives involving voting rights, freedom of speech, reproductive rights, racial justice, LGBTQ+ equality, privacy rights, immigrant rights, criminal justice reform, and many other civil liberties issues. The successful candidate will join the Strategy Office , a department within the Executive Division responsible for shaping the organization s long-term strategic direction, supporting leadership decision-making, and ensuring the successful implementation of the ACLU s ambitious 2030 Strategic Plan . About the Position The Strategy Innovation Advisor will report directly to the Director of Strategy and Innovation while collaborating with other members of the Strategy Office and senior organizational leadership. This role is ideal for professionals who enjoy solving complex organizational challenges, coordinating large cross-functional initiatives, conducting strategic research, and transforming innovative ideas into impactful projects. Because the Strategy Office works closely with executive leadership, the successful candidate will regularly participate in confidential strategic discussions and contribute to organization-wide planning and decision-making. Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will perform a wide range of strategic and operational responsibilities, including: 1. Leading Special Projects The Strategy Innovation Advisor will: Develop innovative organizational initiatives in partnership with the Chief Strategy Officer. Manage projects from concept development through implementation and evaluation. Coordinate projects involving multiple departments and senior leaders. Produce high-quality reports, presentations, strategic briefs, and executive materials. Ensure projects are completed on time while maintaining exceptional attention to detail. 2. Future Forecasting and Strategic Analysis An important aspect of the role involves helping organizational leadership anticipate future challenges and opportunities. Responsibilities include: Analyzing national and global trends affecting civil rights and civil liberties. Preparing strategic forecasting reports for executive leadership and the Board. Organizing the quarterly Leadership Insights speaker series. Identifying nationally recognized experts to engage senior leadership. Facilitating strategic discussions and documenting key organizational insights. 3. Supporting the 2030 Strategic Plan The successful candidate will play a major role in implementing the ACLU s long-term strategy by: Supporting organizational and departmental strategic planning. Conducting qualitative and quantitative research. Analyzing internal organizational data. Reviewing survey findings and policy research. Preparing strategic reports for executive decision-making. Monitoring organizational progress through quarterly and annual evaluations. 4. Leadership Engagement The Strategy Innovation Advisor will also coordinate several leadership events, including: Quarterly Strategy Office retreats. Annual senior leadership retreats. Board planning meetings. Development of meeting agendas. Facilitation of strategy sessions. Coordination of external guest speakers. Desired Skills and Qualifications The ACLU is seeking candidates who demonstrate: Outstanding project management skills. Excellent organizational and planning abilities. Strong written and verbal communication. Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including pivot tables, multi-step formulas, financial models, and data management. Strong Microsoft Word and PowerPoint proficiency. Excellent analytical thinking. Ability to synthesize complex information into executive-level recommendations. Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced and high-impact work environment. Salary and Benefits The ACLU offers an attractive compensation package designed to support employee well-being and professional growth. Benefits include: Annual salary of $142,694 (New York rate). Hybrid working arrangement. Generous paid time off. Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance. Paid parental leave. Gender-affirming healthcare. Fertility treatment benefits. 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching. Annual professional development funding. Internal training programmes and career development workshops. Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion The ACLU is deeply committed to creating an inclusive workplace that values diversity, accessibility, equity, and belonging. The organization strongly encourages applications from qualified candidates of all backgrounds regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, veteran status, age, or other protected characteristics. Reasonable accommodations are available throughout the recruitment process for applicants with disabilities. Why You Should Apply This position provides a unique opportunity to work alongside some of the nation s leading civil rights experts while helping shape the long-term strategy of one of America s most influential advocacy organizations. Professionals interested in public policy, nonprofit leadership, law, business strategy, social justice, organizational innovation, and executive leadership will gain invaluable experience that can open doors to senior leadership roles in government, international organizations, foundations, consulting firms, and nonprofit institutions. Application Deadline Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. However, priority consideration will be given to candidates who submit their applications by 12 July 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of this opportunity. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their application through the official ACLU Careers portal and ensure all required documents are uploaded before the priority deadline. title: African Futures Young Changemakers Initiative 2026: Call for Young Africans to Share Bold Ideas and Win Recognition at the African Futures Conference in South Africa
summary: Applications are now open for the Young Changemakers Initiative 2026 , an exciting continental competition that seeks to identify and celebrate Africa s next generation of visionary leaders, innovators, researchers, entrepreneurs, creatives, community leaders, and storytellers. Organized by the African Futures Innovation (AFI) Programme at the Institute for Security Studies (ISS) , the initiative forms part of the African Futures Conference 2026 , one of the continent s leading platforms for foresight, innovation, and long-term strategic thinking. This initiative provides a unique opportunity for young Africans to contribute their ideas on the future of the continent while gaining continental recognition for their creativity, leadership potential, and commitment to positive change. Successful participants will have the chance to present their ideas to policymakers, researchers, development practitioners, business leaders, and fellow innovators during the African Futures Conference scheduled for 13–14 October 2026 . About the Young Changemakers Initiative Africa is experiencing rapid demographic growth, technological transformation, urbanization, climate challenges, and evolving governance systems. The Young Changemakers Initiative encourages young people to think beyond today s challenges and envision the Africa they want to see in the coming decades. The 2026 edition is built around the inspiring theme: The Choices That Will Shape Africa s Future. Rather than focusing only on present-day issues, participants are encouraged to imagine Africa in 2050 and identify the decisions that governments, businesses, communities, and young people must make today to achieve that future. The organizers believe that Africa s future is not predetermined. Instead, it will be shaped by the collective choices made across sectors including governance, education, technology, economic transformation, peace and security, environmental sustainability, and social development. Questions Participants Can Explore Applicants may choose to answer one or more of the following guiding questions: What kind of Africa would you like to see by 2050? Which decisions today will determine whether that future becomes reality? What opportunities should Africa embrace? What risks should the continent avoid? How can governments, businesses, researchers, civil society, and communities contribute to a better future? What role should Africa s young people play in shaping tomorrow s continent? Submission Formats The competition welcomes different forms of creativity and communication. Participants may submit their ideas in one of the following formats: Written Submission Maximum of 1,500 words PDF or Microsoft Word format Video Submission Between 3 and 5 minutes MP4 format Audio or Podcast Submission Between 3 and 5 minutes MP3 or equivalent format This flexible approach allows participants to communicate their ideas using the format that best reflects their strengths and creativity. Benefits of Participating The Young Changemakers Initiative is more than a competition—it is an opportunity to gain continental visibility and become part of an influential network of future African leaders. Successful participants will enjoy several benefits, including: Recognition as one of Africa s emerging changemakers. Opportunity to showcase innovative ideas on a continental platform. Exposure to policymakers, researchers, academics, innovators, and development partners. Networking opportunities with experts in African development and foresight. Professional recognition that can strengthen future scholarship, fellowship, and career applications. Participation in conversations shaping Africa s long-term development. A panel of experts will select the Top 20 Young Changemakers 2026 , recognizing participants whose submissions demonstrate originality, creativity, leadership potential, and futures-oriented thinking. From these twenty outstanding young leaders, five finalists will receive the Young Changemakers Award and travel support to attend the African Futures Conference 2026 in Pretoria, South Africa , where they will have the opportunity to engage directly with leading thinkers and decision-makers from across the continent. How Entries Will Be Evaluated Submissions will be assessed based on several criteria, including: Originality and creativity. Relevance to Africa s future. Long-term and futures-oriented thinking. Leadership potential. Commitment to positive change. Potential impact. Quality of storytelling and presentation. Ability to inspire meaningful conversations about Africa s future. Important Dates Applicants should note the following timeline: Submission Deadline: 26 July 2026 Top 20 Young Changemakers Announced: 7 August 2026 Top Five Finalists Announced: 19 August 2026 African Futures Conference: 13–14 October 2026 (Hybrid format) The conference will be held with Day One taking place in person at the CSIR Conference Centre in Pretoria, South Africa, with live streaming available , while Day Two will be conducted entirely online , enabling participation from across the continent. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare the following before submitting their application: Title of the submission. Abstract (maximum 250 words). Short biography. Written, video, or audio submission. Applications must be submitted through the official online application portal before the deadline. Get more information about this opportunity on the official website Application Deadline The deadline for submitting entries is 26 July 2026 at 23:59 . Young Africans with bold ideas about the continent s future are encouraged to apply early and take advantage of this unique opportunity to contribute to shaping Africa s long-term development while gaining recognition on one of the continent s premier futures platforms. Discover more global competition opportunities on OFY here The post African Futures Young Changemakers Initiative 2026: Call for Young Africans to Share Bold Ideas and Win Recognition at the African Futures Conference in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Fellow – Refugee Youth Transitions, Belonging, Education and Service Navigation in Canada 2026 | SSHRC-Funded UnborderED Knowledge Project
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Postdoctoral Fellow – Refugee Youth Transitions, Belonging, Education and Service Navigation position under the SSHRC-funded UnborderED Knowledge Project in Canada. Hosted by Access Alliance Multicultural Health and Community Services in Toronto, this unique opportunity is designed for scholars with lived experience of forced migration who are passionate about equity-centred research, participatory arts-based methodologies, refugee education systems, and community-engaged knowledge mobilization. This one-year part-time fellowship offers selected candidates the opportunity to lead a national participatory arts-based research project focused on the educational experiences, belonging, and future pathways of university students in Canada who arrived through humanitarian migration pathways. The position is grounded in social justice, anti-racism, trauma-informed research, and community-based collaboration, making it an outstanding opportunity for researchers seeking meaningful impact beyond academia. About the UnborderED Knowledge Project The UnborderED Knowledge Project is a SSHRC-funded initiative focused on displaced students and researchers, educational access, systems navigation, belonging, and policy innovation in Canada. The selected Postdoctoral Fellow will work closely with Access Alliance Multicultural Health and Community Services and collaborate with interdisciplinary advisors, institutional partners, community organizations, and sector leaders. Although the fellowship is housed in Toronto, Ontario, the project will be conducted virtually due to its national scope. The fellowship strongly values lived experience as research expertise and recognizes the knowledge and perspectives of individuals with forced migration backgrounds as central to ethical and impactful research practice. Fellowship Overview Host Organization Access Alliance Multicultural Health and Community Services Project SSHRC-Funded “UnborderED Knowledge” Project Position Title Postdoctoral Fellow – Refugee Youth Transitions, Belonging, Education and Service Navigation Location Toronto, Ontario, Canada (Virtual National Project) Appointment Duration 1-Year Part-Time Appointment Salary and Benefits 0.6 FTE – CAD $33,000 plus benefits Application Deadline July 17, 2026 Fellowship Start Date August 10, 2026 Research Focus Areas The selected fellow will lead a focused national study exploring: Experiences of displaced students in post-secondary education in Canada Educational access and barriers faced by refugee-background students Belonging, participation, and inclusion within university environments Facilitators and challenges related to completing higher education Future educational and professional pathways for displaced learners Actionable policy recommendations for post-secondary institutions Systems navigation and institutional support for students arriving through humanitarian pathways The research specifically focuses on university students who entered Canada as: Resettled refugees Refugee claimants Individuals arriving through humanitarian migration pathways Key Responsibilities 1. Research Leadership The Postdoctoral Fellow will play a major leadership role in the design and implementation of the research project. Responsibilities include: Designing and implementing qualitative arts-based research methodologies Leading participatory-action research activities Recruiting and engaging student participants across Canada Training student participants in arts-based research methods Developing ethical research protocols Applying trauma-informed and anti-racist research strategies Supporting community-engaged and equity-centred research processes Conducting virtual research and collaborative activities nationwide Applying for ethics approvals and additional grant opportunities Managing data collection, analysis, interpretation, and dissemination Scholarly Contributions The selected fellow will contribute to significant scholarly and academic outputs, including: Completing manuscripts for academic publication Collaborating on scholarly articles with institutional and cluster partners Participating in conference presentations Contributing to interdisciplinary research outputs Supporting SSHRC reporting and synthesis activities Knowledge Mobilization and Policy Engagement A major component of this fellowship involves transforming research findings into practical and accessible knowledge products. Responsibilities include: Developing policy briefs and recommendations Creating slide decks and educational resources Producing accessible infographics and communication materials Presenting findings to institutional leaders and service providers Engaging with community organizations and refugee-serving sectors Contributing to systems-level policy innovation Community and Sector Collaboration The fellowship emphasizes strong collaboration between academia, community organizations, and policy stakeholders. The selected fellow will: Collaborate with Access Alliance and Cluster 2 research teams Participate in interdisciplinary advisory meetings Engage with post-secondary institutions and community organizations Deliver workshops and presentations for student participants Support participatory-action research activities Work with government representatives and sector leaders Required Qualifications Applicants must meet the following essential criteria: Essential Requirements Lived experience of forced migration Legally entitled to work in Canada Must reside in Canada throughout the employment period Demonstrated experience in arts-based research Graduate degree required (PhD preferred) Preferred Qualifications Additional qualifications considered assets include: Experience working or volunteering with immigrant or refugee-serving organizations Experience in qualitative research methodologies Participatory-action research experience Strong academic or policy-oriented writing background Published or publishable scholarly work Commitment to equity and anti-racist approaches Trauma-informed research expertise Strong collaboration and interpersonal skills Experience facilitating virtual workshops Multilingual abilities Lived Experience as Foundational Research Expertise One of the most distinctive features of this fellowship is its recognition of lived experience as foundational scholarly expertise. The project explicitly values lived experience of forced migration as central to: Ethical research design Knowledge interpretation Community engagement Participatory methodologies Policy and systems innovation Knowledge mobilization strategies Applicants are encouraged to explain how their lived experiences shape their research perspectives, ethical commitments, and approaches to community-engaged scholarship. Mentorship and Professional Development Opportunities Selected fellows will receive extensive mentorship and career development support. This includes: Direct mentorship from Dr. Akm Alamgir at Access Alliance Academic mentorship from Dr. Michaela Hynie and institutional collaborators Opportunities to engage with interdisciplinary advisory committees Exposure to policy leaders and government representatives Opportunities to lead and co-author publications Career development and academic networking support Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following materials: Cover Letter (Maximum 2 Pages) Research interests Fit with the fellowship project Relevant experience and motivation Statement on Lived Experience Description of forced migration experience Relevance to research approach and ethics Curriculum Vitae (CV) Writing Sample Academic or policy-oriented sample Three References Contact information required Important Dates Application Deadline: July 17, 2026 Fellowship Start Date: August 10, 2026 Why This Fellowship Matters The Postdoctoral Fellow – Refugee Youth Transitions, Belonging, Education and Service Navigation position represents an important step toward inclusive and community-driven research leadership in Canada. By centering the voices and experiences of displaced students and refugee-background scholars, the UnborderED Knowledge Project aims to transform educational systems, strengthen support structures, and generate actionable solutions that improve educational equity and belonging. This fellowship is ideal for researchers committed to social justice, refugee education, participatory arts-based methods, and policy transformation within higher education systems. Applicants with strong lived experience perspectives, interdisciplinary research interests, and a passion for community-engaged scholarship are strongly encouraged to apply. How to Apply Applicants must submit all required application materials directly to the Hiring Committee at Access Alliance Multicultural Health and Community Services. Submit Applications To: Hiring Committee, Post-Doc Researcher Access Alliance Multicultural Health and Community Services Email: research@accessalliance.ca Applicants should ensure that all required documents are included in their submission before the application deadline of July 17, 2026. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the SSHRC-Funded UnborderED Knowledge Project, and visit the official website HERE . Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Postdoctoral Fellow – Refugee Youth Transitions, Belonging, Education and Service Navigation in Canada 2026 | SSHRC-Funded UnborderED Knowledge Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Google UK Student AI Hackathon 2026: Build AI Applications, Win Google Prizes and Connect with Google Engineers
summary: Google UK has officially opened registrations for the Student AI Hackathon 2026 , an exciting in-person event designed to bring together talented university students from across the United Kingdom to develop innovative Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions while gaining hands-on experience with Google s cutting-edge AI technologies. The one-day hackathon offers students an exceptional opportunity to showcase their technical abilities, collaborate with peers, network with Google engineers, and compete for exciting prizes. Whether you are passionate about machine learning, generative AI, software engineering, or product development, this event provides an ideal platform to transform innovative ideas into real-world AI applications. Organized by Google UK, the Student AI Hackathon aims to help students prepare for future careers in technology by exposing them to practical AI development, collaborative problem-solving, and product innovation. Participants will work in teams to design and build AI-powered applications before presenting their solutions to a panel of judges. About the Google UK Student AI Hackathon Artificial Intelligence continues to reshape industries around the world, creating new opportunities for innovation across healthcare, education, finance, cybersecurity, transportation, sustainability, and countless other sectors. Recognizing the importance of developing future AI talent, Google has created this hackathon to give university students practical experience using Google s AI ecosystem while strengthening their technical and professional skills. Participants will spend the day designing, developing, testing, and presenting an AI application built using a programming language of their choice together with Google s AI tools. The event encourages creativity, teamwork, and practical problem-solving while exposing students to industry-standard technologies used by one of the world s leading technology companies. What Participants Will Do During the hackathon, students will: Form collaborative teams. Design an original AI-powered solution. Build a working application using Google s AI tools. Present their solution before a judging panel. Demonstrate technical implementation and business potential. Network with Google representatives and fellow students. The event is designed to simulate real-world product development by encouraging participants to combine technical excellence with creativity and user-focused design. Judging Criteria Projects will be evaluated across several important areas, including: Teamwork Product design Innovation Technical implementation Presentation quality Business feasibility Overall impact This balanced evaluation ensures that participants are rewarded not only for coding ability but also for creativity, communication, and practical problem-solving skills. Benefits of Participating Students selected for the Google UK Student AI Hackathon will enjoy numerous benefits, including: Hands-on experience building AI applications. Exposure to Google s latest AI technologies. Networking with Google engineers and recruiters. Collaboration with talented students from universities across the UK. Opportunity to strengthen practical programming skills. Experience working on real-world AI challenges. Chance to present ideas before experienced judges. Fantastic Google prizes for winning teams. Exclusive Google merchandise ( Google swag ) for participants. Valuable experience that strengthens CVs, graduate job applications, internships, and future technology careers. Why You Should Apply Hackathons have become one of the best ways for students to demonstrate practical skills beyond the classroom. Employers increasingly value candidates who can collaborate effectively, think creatively under pressure, communicate technical ideas clearly, and rapidly develop working solutions. Participating in a Google-hosted hackathon provides exposure to industry practices while allowing students to build an impressive portfolio project that can be showcased during internship and graduate recruitment processes. Whether your career goal is software engineering, artificial intelligence, machine learning, product management, cybersecurity, or entrepreneurship, this experience can significantly strengthen your professional profile. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Computer Science students. Artificial Intelligence students. Software Engineering students. Data Science students. Machine Learning enthusiasts. Information Technology students. Engineering students with programming experience. Students interested in AI product development. Event Highlights Participants can expect: Team-based AI development. Practical coding sessions. Technical mentorship. Networking opportunities. Live project demonstrations. Prize competition. Google-branded gifts. Career development experience. Application Deadline Google reviews registrations while spaces remain available. As places are limited, interested students are strongly encouraged to register as early as possible to secure participation. Applicants should check the official event page for the latest availability and registration updates. How to Apply Students can register through Google s official event page : Applicants should carefully review the eligibility requirements and event information before completing their registration. title: Green Climate Fund (GCF) Careers 2026: Fully Paid Internships and Specialist Job Opportunities in South Korea for Climate, Policy, Finance, and Development Professionals
summary: The Green Climate Fund (GCF) has announced multiple exciting career opportunities for 2026 at its headquarters in Songdo, Incheon, Republic of Korea . These vacancies include two fully paid internships and one senior specialist position, offering professionals and graduate students the opportunity to contribute directly to one of the world s most influential international climate finance institutions. As the world s largest dedicated multilateral climate fund, the Green Climate Fund plays a central role in supporting developing countries in addressing climate change through transformational investments, climate finance, policy development, monitoring, evaluation, and sustainable development initiatives. Working at GCF provides exposure to international climate policy, multilateral governance, development finance, evaluation, monitoring, and project implementation while collaborating with experts from nearly 80 nationalities. Whether you are a graduate student seeking international internship experience or an experienced professional looking to shape climate finance investments across Africa, these opportunities provide an outstanding platform to build an impactful career in global climate action. This comprehensive guide explains each opportunity, eligibility requirements, responsibilities, benefits, deadlines, and how to apply. About the Green Climate Fund (GCF) Established in 2010 under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) , the Green Climate Fund is the world s largest dedicated multilateral climate fund. The organization supports developing countries by financing projects that: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions Strengthen climate resilience Promote sustainable development Support vulnerable communities Accelerate low-emission development pathways The Fund contributes directly to implementing the objectives of: The Paris Agreement The UNFCCC Global climate adaptation strategies Climate resilience initiatives Since its establishment, GCF has: Approved over 350 climate projects Committed more than USD 20 billion in climate finance Supported projects across more than 130 developing countries Built extensive readiness programmes that strengthen countries capacity to address climate change Headquartered in Songdo, Incheon, South Korea, GCF employs professionals from diverse academic, governmental, private sector, nonprofit, and scientific backgrounds. Available Green Climate Fund Opportunities The Green Climate Fund is currently recruiting for the following positions: IEU Policy Intern Department of Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (DMEL) Intern Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist – Department of Africa Region Each role offers unique responsibilities and professional development opportunities. Opportunity 1: IEU Policy Intern Internship Overview The Independent Evaluation Unit (IEU) is recruiting a Policy Intern to support its Policy and Board Engagement Team. This internship provides a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of: Climate policy Climate finance Independent evaluation International governance Multilateral development institutions Interns will contribute directly to policy analysis and evaluation activities supporting GCF s Board and institutional decision-making. Internship Details Duration: Six months Start Date: September 2026 Location: Songdo, Incheon, Republic of Korea Remuneration: USD 1,600 per month Possibility of renewal Key Responsibilities Successful interns will assist the Policy and Board Engagement Team by: Conducting desk reviews of evaluation policies Benchmarking policy frameworks across international organizations Supporting reviews of the GCF Evaluation Policy Conducting policy analysis for independent evaluations Supporting all phases of GCF Board meetings Maintaining institutional knowledge management repositories Tracking Board decisions and evaluation recommendations Preparing policy briefs and analytical documents Designing PowerPoint presentations Providing administrative and coordination support Performing additional analytical and operational assignments Eligibility Requirements Applicants should: Hold or be completing a Master s or PhD in: Law Public Policy Political Science International Development Environmental Studies Related disciplines Demonstrate strong qualitative research skills Have excellent analytical abilities Possess excellent English communication skills Demonstrate strong writing skills Work effectively in multicultural environments Exercise initiative and intellectual curiosity Maintain confidentiality Preferred Qualifications Preference will be given to candidates with: Experience in independent evaluation Knowledge of multilateral development banks Familiarity with UNFCCC processes Understanding of climate finance Knowledge of environmental policy Experience with Canva, Prezi, or Power BI Learn More Here Opportunity 2: Department of Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (DMEL) Intern Internship Overview The Department of Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (DMEL) seeks an intern to support learning initiatives and knowledge management activities. Interns will contribute to climate knowledge generation, partnerships, research, communications, and institutional learning. Internship Details Duration: Six months Start Date: September 2026 Location: Songdo, Incheon, Republic of Korea Monthly Remuneration: USD 1,600 Responsibilities Interns will support: Knowledge product development Climate impact research Benchmarking studies Surveys Multimedia content Visual knowledge products Learning systems External partnerships Speaker series Internal communities of practice Research drafting Climate impact networks Internal collaboration Strategic departmental priorities Eligibility Requirements Applicants should be enrolled in a Master s programme in: International Relations Social Sciences Law Finance Economics Science Related fields Candidates should also possess: Strong analytical skills Experience solving complex problems Excellent communication skills Strong presentation abilities Project coordination experience Attention to detail Ability to meet deadlines Fluency in English Preferred Qualifications Additional advantages include: Climate finance knowledge Climate change experience Project implementation experience Knowledge of developing countries French Spanish Portuguese Experience using Asana or project management software Learn More Here Opportunity 3: Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist – Department of Africa Region Position Overview The Green Climate Fund is recruiting an experienced Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist to support investments across Africa. The specialist will assess climate investment proposals while supporting economic analysis, financial structuring, investment appraisal, and project negotiations. Job Location Songdo, Incheon Republic of Korea Main Responsibilities The successful candidate will: Investment Assessment Review Concept Notes Assess Funding Proposals Review financial structures Evaluate macroeconomic analyses Assess financial instruments Confirm economic benefits Policy Support Contribute to investment policies Develop financial guidance Produce sector knowledge products Support project appraisal tools Stakeholder Engagement Advise project teams Network with partners Coordinate with external stakeholders Negotiate funded activity agreements Project Delivery Support public sector investments Improve project quality Develop project assessment tools Enhance financial analysis methodologies Qualifications Applicants should possess: Master s degree in: Economics Finance Sustainable Development Climate Policy Environmental Sciences Law Related disciplines Alternatively: Bachelor s degree plus two additional years of relevant experience Applicants must also have: Minimum nine years of relevant professional experience Experience in project finance Financial analysis expertise Climate investment experience International work experience Strong analytical skills Excellent communication abilities Fluency in English Desirable Qualifications Preference will be given to applicants with: Financial sector experience Experience working in Africa French language skills Multi-sector investment experience Relevant sectors include: Energy Climate finance Water Forests Agriculture Urban infrastructure Ecosystems Livelihoods Learn More Here Why Work at the Green Climate Fund? Working with GCF provides opportunities to: Influence global climate policy Support international climate finance Work with leading experts Build international networks Gain multilateral institutional experience Contribute to sustainable development Support vulnerable countries Strengthen professional expertise in climate governance Employees work alongside professionals representing nearly 80 nationalities across academia, government, civil society, international organizations, and the private sector. Click HERE for more information about the Green Climate Fund Careers. Application Deadlines Applicants should note the following deadlines: IEU Policy Intern Deadline: 22 July 2026 DMEL Intern Deadline: 12 July 2026 Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist Deadline: 12 July 2026 (11:59 PM KST) Late applications may not be considered. Diversity and Equal Opportunity The Green Climate Fund strongly values diversity and inclusion. Applications are particularly encouraged from: Women Nationals of developing countries Individuals from diverse professional backgrounds Applicants committed to international climate action Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Is the internship paid? Yes. Both internship positions provide a monthly remuneration of USD 1,600 . Where are the positions based? All positions are based in Songdo, Incheon, Republic of Korea . How long do the internships last? Each internship lasts six months , beginning in September 2026 , with the IEU internship offering the possibility of renewal. Do I need to be enrolled in a Master s programme? For the DMEL Internship, applicants must be enrolled in a Master s programme. For the IEU Internship, applicants should hold or be completing a Master s or PhD. Is English required? Yes. Excellent written and spoken English is essential for all positions. Are applications from developing countries encouraged? Yes. The Green Climate Fund strongly encourages applications from nationals of developing countries, as well as women and candidates from diverse backgrounds. Can experienced professionals apply? Yes. The Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist role is intended for experienced professionals with at least nine years of relevant experience. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the Official Green Climate Fund Careers Portal before the relevant application deadline. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their application materials early, ensuring that all educational qualifications, professional experience, and supporting documents are complete before submission. Conclusion The Green Climate Fund s 2026 recruitment presents an outstanding opportunity for graduate students, early-career professionals, and experienced climate finance specialists to contribute to one of the world s most influential international climate organizations. Whether through the IEU Policy Internship, the Department of Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning Internship, or the Economic and Finance Structuring Specialist position, successful applicants will gain invaluable experience in climate policy, finance, governance, monitoring, evaluation, and sustainable development while working in an international, multicultural environment. With competitive remuneration, meaningful responsibilities, and the opportunity to contribute directly to global climate action and the implementation of the Paris Agreement, these positions offer an exceptional career pathway for individuals passionate about sustainable development and climate resilience. Interested applicants should carefully review the requirements for their preferred position and submit their applications before the applicable deadlines to maximize their chances of joining the Green Climate Fund s global team. Discover more global Internship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY. title: Australian Local Conference of Youth (LCOY) 2026: Young Australians Invited to Shape Global Climate Action Ahead of COP31 Türkiye
summary: Young Australians passionate about climate action now have an exciting opportunity to make their voices heard on one of the world’s most urgent issues. Applications and expressions of interest are now open for the Australian Local Conference of Youth (LCOY) 2026, a youth-led national climate initiative that brings together emerging climate leaders, advocates, students, and changemakers from across the country. Organised by Australian Youth For International Climate Engagement Incorporated (AYFICE) and UN Youth Australia, and supported by UNICEF Australia, the 2026 Australian LCOY aims to empower young people aged 18 to 30 to actively contribute to national and global climate discussions ahead of COP31 Türkiye. The initiative creates a platform where youth can network, discuss climate-related challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and help shape the National Children and Youth Statement, which contributes directly to the Global Youth Climate Statement presented to world leaders during the United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP31). What is the Australian Local Conference of Youth (LCOY)? The Local Conference of Youth (LCOY) is a youth-focused climate conference initiative held in countries around the world under the umbrella of YOUNGO, the official youth constituency of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). LCOY serves as a platform for children and young people to: Discuss pressing climate change issues Share personal experiences and community challenges Build networks with other climate-conscious youth Develop policy recommendations and climate solutions Influence national and international climate negotiations The Australian edition of LCOY is entirely youth-led. Young people are involved in every aspect of the conference process, including: Consultation planning Facilitation and moderation Event logistics Community engagement Consolidation of recommendations Statement drafting and policy writing This ensures that the perspectives presented at international climate negotiations genuinely reflect the concerns and aspirations of Australian youth. Why LCOY 2026 Matters COP31 comes at a critical moment in the global climate crisis. With Australia playing a significant leadership role in international climate negotiations, youth participation has become more important than ever. LCOY 2026 aims to strengthen youth engagement by creating spaces where young Australians can meaningfully contribute to climate policy conversations and influence decision-makers at both national and global levels. Participants will have the opportunity to: Connect with like-minded young climate advocates Participate in meaningful climate discussions Share lived experiences and local environmental concerns Contribute ideas for climate adaptation and mitigation Help shape the Global Youth Climate Statement Gain exposure to international climate diplomacy processes Develop leadership and advocacy skills Join a growing network of youth climate leaders across Australia Australian LCOY 2026 Consultation Dates Several consultations are already scheduled across Australia, including: Perth Consultation Date: 10 July 2026 Time: 5:30 PM – 7:30 PM Sydney Consultation Date: 18 July 2026 Time: 11:30 AM – 2:15 PM Additional consultations may be organised across other Australian states and territories over the coming months. Who Can Apply? The Australian LCOY 2026 consultations are open to: Young Australians aged 18–30 Individuals passionate about climate action Students, activists, advocates, researchers, and emerging leaders Young people interested in sustainability, environmental justice, policy, and international cooperation Applicants do not necessarily need prior experience in climate advocacy. Enthusiasm, curiosity, and willingness to contribute to climate discussions are strongly encouraged. What Participants Will Gain Young people who participate in LCOY 2026 can expect numerous personal and professional development opportunities, including: Networking Opportunities Participants will meet young leaders, activists, policymakers, and climate advocates from different backgrounds and regions across Australia. Leadership Development LCOY provides a practical environment for participants to strengthen leadership, public speaking, collaboration, and advocacy skills. Global Climate Engagement Contributions from Australian consultations will feed into the Global Youth Climate Statement presented during COP31 Türkiye, giving participants a chance to influence global climate discussions. Community Impact Participants will gain tools and knowledge to support climate action initiatives within their local communities. Exposure to International Processes Attendees will learn more about UN climate negotiations, international environmental governance, and youth participation mechanisms within the UNFCCC framework. The Role of UNICEF Australia and Partner Organisations The 2026 Australian LCOY is supported by several major youth and international organisations committed to climate action and youth empowerment. UNICEF Australia UNICEF Australia supports initiatives that amplify youth voices and ensure children and young people are included in policy and decision-making processes related to climate change. Australian Youth For International Climate Engagement (AYFICE) AYFICE focuses on strengthening Australian youth engagement in international climate diplomacy and sustainability discussions. UN Youth Australia UN Youth Australia empowers young Australians through leadership programs, policy engagement, and global citizenship initiatives. Together, these organisations are helping create inclusive opportunities for young Australians to participate in meaningful climate dialogue and policy development. Previous Australian LCOY Initiatives Australian LCOY 2025 In 2025, youth climate leaders conducted 29 consultations across Australia and two online surveys, resulting in the development of a National Children and Youth Statement. Australian LCOY 2024 The 2024 edition included seven face-to-face consultations nationwide, supported by UNICEF Australia, AYFICE, and Plan International Australia. Australian LCOY 2023 The 2023 consultations were conducted in partnership with UNICEF Australia and the Monash Sustainable Development Institute (MSDI), involving youth-led climate conversations across five locations. These previous editions demonstrate the growing momentum of youth climate engagement in Australia and the increasing recognition of youth contributions to climate policymaking. Final Thoughts Climate change remains one of the defining challenges of our generation, and youth voices are becoming increasingly essential in shaping sustainable solutions. The Australian Local Conference of Youth 2026 provides a powerful opportunity for young Australians to contribute directly to national and international climate discussions while building networks, leadership skills, and real-world advocacy experience. For young people passionate about creating a more sustainable future, LCOY 2026 represents more than just a conference — it is a platform to influence policy, inspire communities, and help shape the future of global climate action. Learn More Apply express their interest through the official registration process. Young Australians interested in participating in LCOY 2026 can click HERE to learn more and apply. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Australian Local Conference of Youth (LCOY) 2026: Young Australians Invited to Shape Global Climate Action Ahead of COP31 Türkiye appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Embassy of Denmark in Ghana Recruitment 2026: Communication and Cultural Officer Position Open in Accra
summary: The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana has announced an exciting career opportunity for qualified professionals seeking to contribute to international relations, public diplomacy, cultural engagement, and strategic communications. The Embassy is currently recruiting a Communication and Cultural Officer to join its team in Accra, Ghana. This position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals with strong communication skills, event management expertise, media relations experience, and a passion for cultural diplomacy to work within a respected diplomatic mission. The successful candidate will play a vital role in strengthening Denmark’s visibility and engagement in Ghana while supporting a range of high-profile communication and cultural initiatives. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates must submit their applications by Wednesday, 8 July 2026 . About the Opportunity The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana is seeking a proactive, service-minded, and highly organized professional who can effectively manage communication activities, support cultural programming, and contribute to the Embassy’s public diplomacy efforts. The selected candidate will be responsible for enhancing Denmark’s communication outreach, promoting Danish priorities and partnerships, and increasing public awareness of Denmark’s activities in Ghana and neighboring countries covered by the Embassy. The role combines strategic communication, stakeholder engagement, media management, event coordination, and cultural programming, making it ideal for professionals who enjoy working in a dynamic international environment. Position Details Position Communication and Cultural Officer Employment Type Full-time or part-time Local employment terms and conditions Duty Station 67 Dr. Isert Street North Ridge Accra, Ghana Start Date As soon as possible Application Deadline Wednesday, 8 July 2026 Main Responsibilities The Communication and Cultural Officer will undertake a wide range of duties designed to strengthen Denmark’s diplomatic presence and cultural engagement in Ghana. 1. Support Cultural Projects and High-Level Events The successful candidate will: Lead planning and execution of cultural projects Organize and coordinate high-level events Support official visits and diplomatic engagements Develop initiatives that strengthen Denmark’s image and visibility in Ghana and neighboring accredited countries 2. Manage Media and Public Communication Responsibilities include: Developing communication materials Drafting speeches and talking points Preparing press releases and media content Producing articles and public information materials Supporting strategic media outreach 3. Oversee Digital Communication Channels The officer will be responsible for managing: The Embassy’s communication platforms Official social media accounts Website content and updates Digital communication campaigns Online stakeholder engagement initiatives 4. Build and Maintain Strategic Partnerships The role requires close collaboration with: Journalists and media organizations Cultural institutions International organizations Development partners Communication colleagues within Denmark’s diplomatic network Cultural attachés and embassy representatives from other countries 5. Coordinate Cultural Activities The officer will assist in organizing and managing: Music performances Cultural exhibitions Literary events Arts and cultural showcases Public diplomacy initiatives Cross-cultural engagement activities Required Qualifications and Experience Applicants should possess a strong academic background and relevant professional experience. Educational Requirements Candidates must hold: A bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification in a relevant field, including: Communications Journalism Event Management Public Relations Media Studies Cultural Management Related disciplines Professional Experience Applicants should demonstrate: Experience organizing and managing high-level events Experience coordinating communication campaigns Proven project management skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively Language Requirements Candidates must possess: Excellent written English Excellent spoken English Strong editing and content development skills Essential Competencies The Embassy is seeking individuals with: Strong interpersonal skills Excellent communication abilities Attention to detail Professional judgment Ability to manage multiple priorities Strong stakeholder engagement skills Cross-cultural communication abilities Preferred Qualifications The following will be considered advantages: Knowledge of international affairs Understanding of public diplomacy Experience in cultural programming Interest in international relations Previous work experience within: Embassies International organizations Non-governmental organizations Multicultural institutions Employment Benefits The Embassy offers a competitive employment package, including: Salary and Compensation Salary based on qualifications, relevant experience, and proven work-related achievements Attractive employment conditions under local terms Work Arrangement Full-time or part-time employment options Flexible work schedule possibilities Leave Benefits Employees receive: Five weeks of annual leave Additional leave days equivalent to five workdays Mutual paid leave arrangement totaling three months Health Coverage The Embassy provides: Health care insurance coverage for employees Health care insurance coverage for dependent family members where applicable Professional Environment Successful candidates will benefit from: International work exposure Engagement with diplomatic stakeholders Opportunities to contribute to cultural diplomacy Experience within a respected European diplomatic mission Security and Administrative Requirements Please note that employment is subject to: Security clearance by Danish authorities Presentation of a valid “No Criminal Record” certificate Applicants should ensure they can meet these requirements before applying. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application in English. The application package must include: A brief cover letter A one-page curriculum vitae (CV) Educational qualifications Recommendations Contact details of two references Submission Method Applications should be submitted electronically in PDF format, either: As separate PDF documents, or Combined into a single PDF file Email Subject Line Applicants should use: Communication and Cultural Officer Application Email accran.recruitment@um.dk Application Deadline Wednesday, 8 July 2026 Late applications may not be considered. Selection Process The recruitment process will involve: Review of submitted applications Shortlisting of qualified candidates Interview invitations for selected applicants Final selection and security clearance procedures Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Interviews are expected to take place approximately two weeks after the application deadline. Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here .
